title: rubus nemorosus var. acutangulus
intro: Rubus nemorosus var. acutangulus, also known as the Black Raspberry Bramble, is a thorny shrub native to Eastern Asia and Japan, characterized by its edible red or black fruits and medicinal properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, fever, and inflammation due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rubus nemorosus var. acutangulus include reducing the risk of heart disease and certain types of cancer, as well as supporting the immune system and promoting digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions made from its leaves, stems, and fruits, which can be used to treat various health conditions when consumed in moderation.
